metadata.description: Sulis AI plugin marketplace — methodology studios, requirements analysis, facilitation, context cartography, right-sized architecture, disciplined change primitives, deterministic code intelligence, founder coordination, and autonomous Work Package execution end-to-end. v1.56.0 rewrites sulis:add-skill to v0.7.0 — standards-grounded methodology citing the five v1.55.0 standards at each of the five gates. Gate 1 (Find) adopts Balanced Investigation / Source Independence / Confidence Calibration + adds Primitive Discovery sub-step grounded in PG-01..04 + PD-01..06. Gate 2 (Scope Lock) adopts STANDARDS_RUBRIC phase classification + Outside-In Reasoning + MECE; new lock items: standards-phase classification, verification tier, tool stack (audit-pattern skills must declare which real tools they invoke — Semgrep / Gitleaks / Trivy / lizard / jscpd / hadolint / testssl / curl — pure-regex requires explicit justification), related skills (per REFERENTIAL_INTEGRITY). Gate 3 (Generate) adopts MECE + Pyramid + SCQA + No Hyperbole linguistic audit. Gate 4 (Evaluate) adopts SPIRAL_TEMPLATES tier + dimensions + Codebase Referential Integrity + VERIFICATION_REPORT.md on disk (single filesystem check determines compliance). Gate 5 (Adversarial) adopts AT-01..03 + Independence Check for HEAVY-tier skills (Agent subagent_type=Explore in fresh context). Skills are deep + thorough, never fast — the fast-vs-deep framing rejected. Three modes: greenfield (new skill), deepening / upsurge (extending an existing skill), standards-grounded re-author (pre-v0.7.0 → v0.7.0). COMPLETENESS_REPORT.md renamed to VERIFICATION_REPORT.md (aligned with SPIRAL_TEMPLATES). v1.55.0 ships five cross-cutting standards inside the sulis plugin (plugins/sulis/references/standards/) — Critical Thinking (13 principles + 9 anti-patterns), Decomposition Procedure (PD-01..PD-06), Spiral Templates (LIGHT/STANDARD/HEAVY tiers + ACCA + Codebase Referential Integrity + Independence Check + VERIFICATION_REPORT.md template), Standards Rubric (input/processing/output/governance phase classification), Referential Integrity (4 canonical relationship types + RI-01..05). Adapted from the platform methodology standards (CRITICAL_THINKING_STANDARD v1.5.0, decomposition-procedure v1.0.0, SPIRAL_TEMPLATES v2.1.0, STANDARDS_RUBRIC v1.0.0, REFERENTIAL_INTEGRITY_STANDARD v1.0.1) and trimmed for sulis scale — Wired Outcomes Registry dropped, 26-standard inventory reduced to 5 sulis-local entries, ACCA inlined into Spiral Templates so the port is self-contained. Foundation for the upsurge plan in /Users/iain/.claude/plans/eager-crunching-quail.md — Phase 1 rewrites add-skill v0.7.0 to cite these standards at each gate (replaces ad-hoc methodology with platform-proven rigor); Phase 2 upsurges each check-* skill against the methodology + scores under the spiral rubric. v1.19.0 adds sulis-business-strategy (Kind-schema variant of the business-strategy studio, v0.1.0 test fixture): ships the Identity Kind — a four-stage Kind manifest (find → generate → evaluate → decide) that replaces the legacy identity-articulation outcome's three-lens triad with a single evaluate stage + rubric + structured Verdict + reason-class routing. The triad's function (multi-perspective evaluation with consensus) is preserved structurally in evaluate + decide; its mechanism (three parallel agent lanes) is replaced. Coexists with the established sulis-strategy plugin. Until the platform engine wires Slices 2-4 of the Kind compiler, the plugin's business-strategist agent drives all four stages manually; when the engine catches up, the agent simplifies to invoking the Kind via KindHandler with no Kind YAML change. Test fixture for promoting the migration pattern to studios. v1.16.0 publishes the Repository Contract Standard (RC-01..RC-13) at plugins/srd/references/repository-contract-standard.md — the marketplace-wide specification of the GitHub repository shape every Sulis agent assumes. Locks: dev/main two-branch model, branch protections, GitHub Merge Queue config on dev (group size 5, concurrency 3, failure tolerance 3 per the Shopify pattern), six required Actions workflows (branch-ci on pull_request, merge-queue-ci on merge_group, deploy-staging on push to dev, health-and-smoke on workflow_run, promote-dev-to-main on workflow_dispatch, release-prod on push to main), staging + production environments, per-env deploy tokens, squash-only repo settings, v* SemVer tag protection, token scopes, and the agent arrival check (executor Step 0) with structured JSON output. Strict mode for v1: agents refuse non-conforming repos and surface a delta list in founder English. Composes with GIT-01..GIT-10 (operationalises the two-branch model on GitHub), EL-01..EL-08 (adds Step 0 to the executor lifecycle), and the cicd-batching-analysis.md research (the merge-queue config encodes its per-batch deploy recommendation). Companion scripts wpx-arrival-check + wpx-bootstrap-repo specified in RC-11 and the Bootstrap Workflow section; implementation scheduled for the next sulis-execution release. The cicd-batching-analysis.md technical research ships alongside at plugins/sulis-execution/docs/research/ documenting the merge-queue convention (Bors → Shopify Shipit → GitHub Merge Queue) and SEA's natural batch unit (DAG topological levels). All v1.15.x capabilities preserved.

plugins.name: srd
plugins.description: Requirements Analyst — facilitates building Software Requirements Documents through guided conversation with UML artifacts. Marketplace home of cross-plugin standards (Founder English, Repository Contract, PR Hygiene, Change Work, Critical Thinking). See CHANGELOG.md for version history.

plugins.name: sulis-context
plugins.description: Context Cartographer — discovers existing architecture documentation, ADRs, conventions, and standards in a project; produces .context/{project}/INDEX.md so downstream plugins (SRD, SEA, sulis-security) respect prior decisions instead of restating them.

plugins.name: sulis-strategy
plugins.description: Business strategy studio for the Outcome-First Methodology. Provides vision, strategy, principles, commercial, gtm-planning, roadmap, identity, brand-research, company-research, competitive-research, anti-goals, and win-loss-analysis skills.

plugins.name: sulis-business-strategy
plugins.description: Business Strategy studio (Kind-schema variant). Drives business-context intake, identity, brand positioning, voice, principles, and strategic foundation work through four-stage Kind workflows (find / generate / evaluate / decide).

plugins.name: sulis-product-development
plugins.description: Product development studio for the Outcome-First Methodology. Provides design, plan, implement, and complete skills for the full product delivery lifecycle. Includes test-scenarios, task-decomposition, backend/frontend development guidance, and IVS-authoring.

plugins.name: sulis-builder
plugins.description: Self-service studio creation for the Outcome-First Methodology. Creates new domain expertise packages (7-file studio bundles) using the studio-creation sequence; outcome-definition + platform integration + tail outcomes.

plugins.name: sulis-design
plugins.description: Design studio for the Outcome-First Methodology. Provides design foundation, visual identity, customer experience, design coherence, design compliance, identity articulation, and implementation system skills for translating brand into a design system.

plugins.name: sulis-platform-sdk
plugins.description: Build production-ready, business-critical applications with AI-native infrastructure. Deploy scalable SaaS backends with built-in auth, billing, multi-tenancy, and observability.

plugins.name: sea
plugins.description: Senior Engineering Architect — designs hardened architectures from SRDs, audits brownfield codebases, decomposes designs into atomic Work Packages with Red-Green-Blue verification. Publishes the Code Review Standard (CR-01..CR-10) and the decompose validation rubric. See CHANGELOG.md for version history.

plugins.name: sulis-security
plugins.description: Security & Viability Reviewer — runs a 25-primitive codebase viability assessment (security, data protection, code quality, supply chain, infrastructure) via an OODA-spiral methodology. Produces evidence-backed reports with scores, attack chains, and hypotheses.

plugins.name: idc
plugins.description: Investor Deck Coach — facilitates Sequoia-style pitch deck creation through guided conversation, stage-aware (angel through Series B), with rigorous market research, financial modelling, adversarial investor-objection review, branded deck deliverables (PowerPoint + Reveal.js HTML), and rehearsal drill.

plugins.name: sulis-execution
plugins.description: Work Package Executor + Resumable Train — atomic per-WP lifecycle (worktree → RGB → Step 6.5 code-review → commit → push) plus a per-batch train (rebase → CI → merge → deploy → Step 10.5 + Step 11 gates). Self-heals via OODA + Five Whys. See CHANGELOG.md for version history.

plugins.name: sulis-concierge
plugins.description: DEPRECATED — the concierge agent + journey skills moved to the `sulis` plugin (the canonical front-door) at v0.2.0. Run `claude --agent sulis` instead of `claude --agent sulis-concierge`; use `/sulis:start`, `/sulis:handoff`, `/sulis:status` in place of the old `/sulis-concierge:*` invocations. This plugin retained as a deprecation shim; remove it when convenient.

plugins.name: sulis
plugins.description: Sulis — the canonical front-door plugin for the Sulis AI marketplace. Hosts the founder-facing concierge agent + journey skills (start/handoff/status), the meta-skill methodology (add-skill) used to author other plugins, and the five cross-cutting standards (Critical Thinking, Decomposition Procedure, Spiral Templates, Standards Rubric, Referential Integrity) at plugins/sulis/references/standards/. Founders interact via /sulis:* commands; specialist domain plugins (srd/sea/sulis-execution/sulis-security/etc) remain operator-facing internals.
